payload.name: Hello,I only have one task for you. I want you to
payload.description: Hello,I only have one task for you. I want you to familiarize yourself with this repo and tell me how many pages are here and what those pages are supposed to be doing.

goal.answer: Familiarize with the PlantPal repo, count all pages, and produce a comprehensive markdown report describing what each page does, its route paths, authentication requirements, key data queries, and any flagged structural issues.

payload.plain: Familiarize with the PlantPal repo, count all pages, and produce a comprehensive markdown report describing what each page does, its route paths, authentication requirements, key data queries, and any flagged structural issues.

criteria.answer:
A markdown report file exists at a known path (e.g., PAGE_INVENTORY.md) containing both a concise bulleted summary and a detailed structured inventory of all pages.
The report accurately counts and distinguishes routed pages (16 routes in App.tsx) from all .tsx page-component files in src/pages/ (18 files), with both numbers explicitly stated.
The report documents, for every routed page: its route path, whether authentication is required (public vs protected), and a description of its intended purpose based on actual code and queries.
The report identifies the key data sources / TanStack Query / Supabase queries associated with each routed page.
The report flags any obvious structural issues discovered during exploration (e.g., unused routes, dead imports, placeholder content, obvious bugs likely visible in code).
Verification by validating the report file exists, grepping for all 18 .tsx filenames inside it, and confirming it has both a summary section and a detailed section.
Verification by spot-checking that descriptions for 3+ sampled pages accurately reflect their code (e.g. CommunityPage has a post feed and create-post sheet, LiveHostPage uses LiveKitRoom).

plain: A markdown report file exists at a known path (e.g., PAGE_INVENTORY.md) containing both a concise bulleted summary and a detailed structured inventory of all pages.
The report accurately counts and distinguishes routed pages (16 routes in App.tsx) from all .tsx page-component files in src/pages/ (18 files), with both numbers explicitly stated.
The report documents, for every routed page: its route path, whether authentication is required (public vs protected), and a description of its intended purpose based on actual code and queries.
The report identifies the key data sources / TanStack Query / Supabase queries associated with each routed page.
The report flags any obvious structural issues discovered during exploration (e.g., unused routes, dead imports, placeholder content, obvious bugs likely visible in code).
Verification by validating the report file exists, grepping for all 18 .tsx filenames inside it, and confirming it has both a summary section and a detailed section.
Verification by spot-checking that descriptions for 3+ sampled pages accurately reflect their code (e.g. CommunityPage has a post feed and create-post sheet, LiveHostPage uses LiveKitRoom).

constraints.answer: Keep the deliverable focused on analysis and reporting only; do not refactor application code., Produce the report inside the repo at a known path so the user can open and read it easily., Do not block on runtime testing or build verification — the task is static analysis.

plain: Keep the deliverable focused on analysis and reporting only; do not refactor application code.
Produce the report inside the repo at a known path so the user can open and read it easily.
Do not block on runtime testing or build verification — the task is static analysis.

phaseSnapshots.name: Synthesize and write inventory report
phaseSnapshots.goal: Consolidate all exploration findings into the final markdown report file with summary, detailed inventory, route/auth table, query mapping, and issue flags.

steps.description: Synthesize subagent findings, orient observations, and App.tsx route table into structured markdown. Write the report with: (a) executive summary with counts, (b) route inventory table (path, page, auth, description, key queries), (c) notes on orphaned/unused pages, (d) flagged issues section.

verification.command: test -f PAGE_INVENTORY.md && grep -q 'PlantPal' PAGE_INVENTORY.md && grep -q '16 routed' PAGE_INVENTORY.md

steps.description: Validate the report covers all 18 .tsx page filenames and all 16 active routes by grepping the file.

assumptions.answer: The user prefers a single markdown document delivered in the repository root.; Index.tsx is an orphaned leftover scaffold page and should be flagged but not removed.; Subagent exploration already covered every page and route in sufficient depth; remaining work is synthesis and writing.; All descriptions should be grounded in the actual code observed by the subagents, not inferred from file names alone.

payload.summary: Phase 1 produced a comprehensive 11KB markdown report (PAGE_INVENTORY.md) covering all 18 page component files, 16 active routes, auth requirements, key data queries, and flagged issues. Step 1 synthesized subagent findings into structured markdown; Step 2 validated that every filename is present and the `src/pages/` reference count exceeds the required threshold. Both verifications passed.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.finalSummary: Ferment completed successfully. Produced PAGE_INVENTORY.md, a comprehensive 11KB markdown report that inventories all 18 page component files in src/pages/, maps the 16 active routes with auth requirements and key data queries, documents the orphaned Index.tsx, and flags 10+ structural/mock-data issues discovered during static analysis.
